title: Don't create non-hidden contexts folder in home folder
body: ### Feature request description All the files at $HOME (~/) directory are essential core stuff. And when all other apps create folders they create with "." at the beginning of the folder name so that they are hidden. But podman creates a `contexts` folder just for the socket. ### Suggest potential solution IMHO I believe it absolutely does not belong in `$HOME` like that. ```bash # Maybe store this somewhere else: ~/.config/podman/contexts/ ~/.config/containers/podman/contexts ~/.config/containers/podman-desktop/contexts ~/.config/containers/contexts/ ~/.docker/contexts ``` ### Have you considered any alternatives? No ### Additional context ```console (base) sglbl@ddspc:~$ sysinfo System: Host: ddspc Kernel: 6.17.0-20-generic arch: x86_64 bits: 64 Desktop: GNOME v: 46.0 Distro: Zorin OS 18.1 noble Session: x11 (base) sglbl@ddspc:~$ podman --version podman version 4.9.3 (base) sglbl@ddspc:~$ cat .bashrc | grep podman export DOCKER_HOST="unix://$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock" ```

comments.body:
I have seen this bug in other parts of Podman Desktop, when variables go missing in the config (maybe due to upgrade)?

It was supposed to go in the DOCKER_CONFIG folder, which is supposed to default to `~/.docker` and not to "" (or $HOME)

```ts
      const metaFilePath = join(dockerContextsMetaPath, contextSha256Name, 'meta.json');
```

```ts
    const dockerContextsMetaPath = join(this.#dockerConfig.getPath(), 'contexts', 'meta');
```

```ts
    return configuration.getConfiguration('docker').get('config') ?? DockerConfig.DEFAULT_CONFIG_FOLDER;
```

```ts
  static readonly DEFAULT_CONFIG_FOLDER = join(homedir(), '.docker');
```

----

EDIT: Looks like a duplicate:

* https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/16433

Podman does not write any contexts directory. If this is a bug with podman-desktop it must be report over there https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop

Looks like @afbjorklund linked an existing bug already so closing this.

The Podman files are in either ~/.config/containers/containers.conf or ~/.config/containers/podman-connections.json depending on your Podman version (since the `docker context` concept is called `podman system connection`)

> Podman does not write any contexts directory. If this is a bug with podman-desktop it must be report over there https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop
> 
> Looks like [@afbjorklund](https://github.com/afbjorklund) linked an existing bug already so closing this.

Thank you. I have both podman and podman desktop so I wasn't sure which one creates this. The issue that's linked is for macos but I commented that it also happens on Linux. Thank you for your help.
